snippets.title: Two-pointer (opposite ends) — two-sum on a sorted array
snippets.explanation: Each iteration advances exactly one pointer; total work is O(n) instead of the O(n²) double loop.

snippets.title: Two-pointer (fast/slow) — in-place dedup of a sorted array
snippets.code:
def dedup_sorted(arr):
    if not arr: return 0
    slow = 0                       # boundary of the unique prefix
    for fast in range(1, len(arr)):
        if arr[fast] != arr[slow]:
            slow += 1
            arr[slow] = arr[fast]
    return slow + 1                # length of the unique prefix

xs = [1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4]
n = dedup_sorted(xs)
print(xs[:n])   # [1, 2, 3, 4]
snippets.explanation: Fast scans the array; slow marks where the next unique value goes. O(n) time, O(1) extra space.

snippets.title: Fixed-size sliding window — rolling average of last k
snippets.explanation: Each value enters and leaves the deque exactly once — total O(n), O(k) extra memory.

snippets.title: Variable-size sliding window — longest substring without repeats
snippets.code:
def longest_unique(s: str) -> int:
    last = {}                                # char → most recent index
    best, l = 0, 0
    for r, ch in enumerate(s):
        if ch in last and last[ch] >= l:
            l = last[ch] + 1                  # shrink past the duplicate
        last[ch] = r
        best = max(best, r - l + 1)
    return best

print(longest_unique('abcabcbb'))    # 3  ("abc")
print(longest_unique('bbbbb'))       # 1
print(longest_unique('pwwkew'))      # 3  ("wke")
snippets.explanation: `r` advances exactly n times; `l` only moves forward — each index moves O(1) times → O(n) total.

snippets.title: Two-pointer over two sequences — merge sorted
snippets.explanation: The merge step of mergesort and many streaming joins. O(m + n).
